meta_title: For Students: API integration guide to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: API Integration Guide to Solve Deployment Errors
content.seo_description: Learn to master API integration and deploy your first AI app with this beginner-friendly checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code. Start building today.
content.answer_block: Deploying fails when API keys are hardcoded, environment variables are missing, or CORS policies block requests. To fix this, decouple your backend, use robust environment management, and validate responses. This guide streamlines your integration workflow, ensuring your AI application moves from your local environment to a live, production-ready state seamlessly.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect AI logic, only to watch your terminal scream red errors the moment you attempt to deploy. The excitement of your local build is replaced by the crushing reality of '403 Forbidden' and '500 Internal Server Error' messages. You aren't failing as a developer; you are simply hitting the wall where local scripts must evolve into production-grade systems.

This is the rite of passage for every AI builder. By mastering API integration, you bridge the gap between a fragile prototype and a robust, scalable application. With the right tools—Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent debugging—we will dismantle these deployment barriers and get your project live.

steps.title: Sanitize Your Credentials
steps.description: Never commit API keys to version control. Move all keys to a .env file and ensure it is listed in your .gitignore to prevent public exposure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current codebase to move all hardcoded API keys into a secure .env configuration pattern using dotenv.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a .env.example file and update the codebase to use process.env variables'

steps.title: Implement Robust Error Handling
steps.description: Don't let a failed API request crash your entire app. Implement try-catch blocks and meaningful fallback states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Wrap all third-party API calls in comprehensive error handling with user-friendly console logs and fallback responses.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'review all API fetch calls and ensure they have error handling and graceful fallbacks'

steps.title: Configure CORS for Production
steps.description: Deployment often fails due to browser security policies. Define your allowed origins explicitly rather than using wildcards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a production-ready CORS configuration that restricts access to my specific domain and handles preflight requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a secure CORS middleware configuration to the server entry point'

steps.title: Validate API Request Payloads
steps.description: Ensure the data sent to your API matches expected schemas to prevent runtime failures during deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Zod schemas for all API inputs to validate request data before sending it to the provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'install zod and implement input validation for all outgoing API requests'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Health Checks
steps.description: Add a simple endpoint that verifies API connectivity so you can confirm the deployment was successful immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a /health-check endpoint that verifies the connectivity and authentication status of all integrated APIs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a health check route that pings external APIs and returns status codes'

pro_tips: Always use a mock-server library like MSW during development to decouple your UI work from API latency.
Implement request logging in production, but ensure you redact sensitive headers like Authorization tokens.
Set up rate-limit alerts in your API provider's dashboard to catch infinite loops before they burn your budget.
Use a dedicated service for environment variable management instead of relying on local files in production environments.
Always test your production build locally using 'npm run build && npm run start' before pushing to your cloud provider.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is stable, focus on implementing CI/CD pipelines to automate your deployment process every time you push to your main branch.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting deployment errors hold your brilliance back. You have the tools, the roadmap, and the expertise to launch your AI vision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: Never commit API keys to version control. Move all keys to a .env file and ensure it is listed in your .gitignore to prevent public exposure.
Don't let a failed API request crash your entire app. Implement try-catch blocks and meaningful fallback states.
Deployment often fails due to browser security policies. Define your allowed origins explicitly rather than using wildcards.
Ensure the data sent to your API matches expected schemas to prevent runtime failures during deployment.
Add a simple endpoint that verifies API connectivity so you can confirm the deployment was successful immediately.
